Brosa Supply Chain
Workshop
Understanding our COGS & Pricing
Richard Li
28th Feb 2017
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 10 --

What is COGS?
• 	“COGS” is abbreviation for Cost of Goods Sold
• 	COGS are the direct costs attributable to the
production of the goods sold by Brosa, it also
includes the inward freight (i.e international
freight) and duty (if applicable).
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 10 --

Trading Terms
• 	EXW: Ex-works
EXW is an international trade term that describes an agreement in which the seller is required to make goods ready
for pickup at his or her own place of business. All other transportation costs and risks are assumed by the buyer.
• 	FOB: Free on Board
FOB is an international trade term that describes an agreement in which the seller is required to make goods ready
and is also obligated to deliver the goods to port. All local transportation costs and risks are carried by the seller.
• 	CIF (or CNF)
Cost, Insurance and Freight, or CIF, is a trade term that means the seller must pay the costs needed to transport
goods to a port of destination. Under this trading term, seller does not cover the destination custom clearance cost
or local cartage.
• 	DDU: Delivered Duty Unpaid
• 	DDP: Delivered Duty Paid
The difference between DDU and DDP terms of delivery can be explained as below: DDU means Delivered Duty
Unpaid. DDP means Delivered Duty Paid. In a DDU shipment, except duty or taxes of importing country, all other
charges has to be paid by the seller of goods.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 10 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 10 --

COGS under Each Term
• 	Under EXW, COGS = Purchase Cost + Origin Transportation &
Custom Clearance + International Freight + Destination
Transportation & Custom Clearance
• 	Under FOB, COGS = Purchase Cost + International Freight +
Destination Transportation & Custom Clearance
• 	Under CIF (or CNF), COGS = Purchase Cost + Destination
Transportation & Custom Clearance
• 	Under DDU, COGS = Purchase Cost + Custom Clearance (inc
Duty if applicable)
• 	Under DDP, COGS = Purchase Cost
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 10 --

FOB -> COGS
• 	Under FOB, COGS = Purchase Cost ($) +
International Freight (V) + Destination
Transportation (V) & Custom Clearance ($)
$ = Correlated to the cost of the product
V = Correlated to the volume of the carton box,
generally measured in CBM (i.e cubic meters)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 10 --

Import Duty & GST
• 	A payment levied on the import of goods
• 	Duty rates in Australia vary from 0% to 10%, with an average duty
rate of 4.6%. Most electronic goods are not subject to duty, but
products like alcohol & cigarettes are subject to very high duty
• 	Duty = AUD (duty rate x FOB)
• 	Import GST = AUD (10% of CIF (or CNF, i.e FOB + International
Freight))
• 	FTA (COO), more info can be found on AusTrade website
http://www.austrade.gov.au/Australian/Export/Free-Trade-Agreements/
ChAFTA
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 10 --

Shipping Containers
• 	One of the most influential inventions in the 20th Century,
it standardised how cargo is shipped in modern days;
• 	The most common ones that Brosa uses are 20ft, 40ft and
40hc containers
20ft = 33CBM
40ft = 67CBM
40hc = 76CBM
It’s approximately 85% usage 	rate for Brosa products
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 10 --

COGS Example
• 	Camden 3 Seater in Cloud Grey, Brosa RRP
$1299
FOB: USD $232, fx rate 0.77
Carton Measurements: 202x92x75cm
40HC Loadability: 76*0.85/(2.02*0.92*0.75) = 46pc
Ocean Freight Cost: $1000 (USD)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 10 --

Pricing Model
• 	GM (Gross Margin): (Total Sales Revenue -
COGS)/Total Sales Revenue expressed as a %.
• 	Brosa’s target GM is between 50% and 70%
depending on the value of the product.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
